steps.title: Fastest path: organize the givens first
steps.content: Track the given side and angle relationships first: equal sides, equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle or side chase once the givens are organized.

steps.title: Recognize the triangle is isosceles
steps.content: We're given $AB = 5{,}740\text{ mm}$ and $BC = 5{,}740\text{ mm}$. Since two sides are equal, triangle $ABC$ is isosceles.

steps.title: Apply the definition of equilateral
steps.content: An equilateral triangle has all three sides equal in length. Since $AB = BC = 5{,}740\text{ mm}$, we need $AC = 5{,}740\text{ mm}$ for the triangle to be equilateral. The credited option provides exactly this: $AC = 5{,}740\text{ mm}$. A is correct.
